name: Potato Soup with Worst and Garden Herbs
recipeIngredient: 4 stalks parsley, finely chopped, about 1/4 cup
4 stalks celery, finely chopped, about 1/4 cup
recipeInstructions:
A Dutch bouillon tablet is good for 1/2 liter (about 2 cups) water; an American cube is good for 1 cup water.
“Cooked worst” is a hot dog, 2–3 inches in diameter. When we get a craving for a bologna sandwich, this is what we use. It doesn’t look like bologna—smaller around and coarser—but the taste is similar. You could substitute good-quality bologna.
Celery; if unavailable, use more parsley.
1. Peel, wash, then dice the potatoes.
2. Put the pots in a pan with the water, bouillon tablet, and wine.
3. Bring to a boil; put a lid on, turn down the heat, and simmer for 20 minutes until the potatoes are done.
4. Let cool briefly.
5. Puree the potatoes and cooking liquid; return to the pan.
6. Warm the soup broth.
7. Stir in the cooked worst and herbs; heat till warmed through.
Serves 2 as main course, or 4–6 as starter.
